title: robinia × bella-rosea
intro: Robinia × bella-rosea, also known as the Robinia pseudoacacia hybrid, is a cross between the Black Locust (Robinia pseudoacacia) and the Rose (Rosa spp.), resulting in a unique ornamental tree with attractive white flowers and pink-tinged foliage. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and inflammation due to its high content of isoflavones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Robinia × bella-rosea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
